$comment: mcp-conformance.v1 — the SINGLE shared rulebook of MCP (2025-06-18) behaviors that EVERY Windy Talk MCP surface must satisfy, regardless of language. control.mcp.v1 (TypeScript, apps/desktop/electron/control/mcp.ts) and hands.mcp.v1 (Python, hands/surface.py) each ship a thin driver that feeds these exact cases to their handler and asserts the result. The behaviors live HERE, in one file both rails read — so the two implementations can never silently drift (which is exactly how the hands str()/initialize gap arose: two separate implementations of the same idea, one got fixed, one didn't). A new surface inherits the whole guarantee by adding a driver. Adding a case tightens BOTH rails at once. $SAFE_READ_TOOL is substituted by each driver with a known safe, side-effect-free read tool on that surface.

$assert_ops.equal: [path, value] — the value at `path` in the response deep-equals `value`.
$assert_ops.type: [path, typename] — the runtime type at `path` is `typename` (object|array|string|boolean|number|null).
$assert_ops.nonempty_array: [path] — the value at `path` is an array with length >= 1.
$assert_ops.structured_matches_text: [] — JSON.parse(result.content[0].text) deep-equals result.structuredContent (canonical-JSON + structuredContent must agree, and the text must be valid JSON).

$path_note: Dotted path into the response; a numeric segment indexes an array (e.g. result.content.0.text). `id` reads the top-level JSON-RPC id.

cases.name: ping returns an empty result object
notifications/initialized gets NO response (it is a notification)
a request method sent WITHOUT an id is a notification: no response, no execution
a batch (JSON array) body is Invalid Request (-32600), not a silent notification
an unknown method returns -32601 and echoes the id
tools/list returns a non-empty array of {name, description, inputSchema}
tools/call returns canonical JSON text AND matching structuredContent (never str()-rendered)
id:0 is a request (falsy but present) — echoed, not dropped
id:null (present) is a request, not a notification — echoed as null
